summary:
The fracture resistance of a gold dental restoration is influenced by its design, the choice of alloy used, including proportions of noble vs. base metals; these elements collectively determine structural integrity under load.
The primary determinants for avoiding fractures include proper restoration engineering, selection of appropriate materials like high-copper or palladium-based alloys, ensuring adequate thickness while maintaining good marginal fit around teeth structures. Agent1 concluded based on multiple studies highlighting critical roles played individually but interdependently among mentioned variables affecting longevity against breakage risks associated specifically during masticatory functions involving such crowns/bridges etc.. 

Note: I've maintained brevity as per your instruction without losing essential details pertinent directly towards addressing core query posed about main influencing factors regarding potential failures due to cracks/breakages observed clinically over time post placement procedures performed using this particular material system commonly employed today globally amongst dentists practicing modern techniques incorporating advanced knowledge bases derived from extensive research conducted worldwide spanning decades now!
